title: Florence 2-Day Cultural Tour
metaTitle: Florence 2-Day Cultural Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to Florence, Italy: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Uffizi Gallery and Accademia Gallery (Michelangelo's David). Prices from $971 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 2 days discovering Florence, Italy — Mediterranean climate with cool, damp winters (highs 11°C) and hot, humid summers (highs 32°C) that can feel stifling in the enclosed city center. April to June and September to October bring warm days, blooming Tuscan countryside and fewer crowds than midsummer. This itinerary blends Uffizi Gallery, Accademia Gallery (Michelangelo's David), Piazzale Michelangelo with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Uffizi Gallery, Accademia Gallery (Michelangelo's David), Piazzale Michelangelo
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Florence
itinerary.description: Land at Florence Airport (Peretola) (FLR) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to Uffizi Gallery — Renaissance masterpieces by Botticelli, Michelangelo and more. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Florence Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to Accademia Gallery (Michelangelo's David) — Home of the original David statue. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to FLR for your onward flight.

travelTips: Book Uffizi and Accademia tickets online well ahead — same-day lines can stretch for hours.
Climb Giotto's Campanile instead of the Duomo dome if the dome is sold out — similar views, less crowded.
Visit Piazzale Michelangelo at sunset but arrive by public bus 12 or 13 to skip the uphill walk.
Try schiacciata (Tuscan flatbread sandwiches) at All'Antico Vinaio for an affordable, iconic lunch.
Combine a Florence base with day trips by train to Pisa or Siena rather than moving hotels.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Modest clothing for church and Duomo visits
Comfortable shoes for cobblestones and hill climbs
A light layer for cool museum interiors
Sun protection for summer piazza walking
thingsToAvoid: Leather stalls at San Lorenzo Market claiming "genuine leather" without verification — quality varies widely.
Restaurants with multilingual tourist menus directly around the Duomo — overpriced, average food.
Touching or leaning on Renaissance artwork/sculptures — heavy fines apply.
Visiting the Uffizi without a reservation in peak season — queues can exceed 3 hours.
Cheap "gelato" with unnaturally bright colors and tall mounds — a sign of low-quality artificial ingredients.
nightlife: Le Volpi e l'Uva wine bar
instagramSpots: Duomo dome view from Giotto's Tower
hiddenGems: Convent of San Marco (Fra Angelico frescoes)
adventureActivities: Via Ferrata day trips near the Apennines
luxuryExperiences: Private Chianti vineyard tour with tastings
Bespoke leather-goods fitting in Oltrarno ateliers
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset at Piazzale Michelangelo with prosecco
Horse-drawn carriage ride through the historic center
budgetExperiences: Free sunset view from Piazzale Michelangelo
Admire the Duomo and Baptistery exteriors for free
Picnic in the Boboli Gardens (grounds fee only)

festivalCalendar.name: Scoppio del Carro (Explosion of the Cart)

localEtiquette: Dress modestly for the Duomo and other churches — shoulders and knees covered.
Order gelato from artisanal ("gelateria artigianale") shops rather than garish, mounded displays aimed at tourists.
Eating on the steps of the Duomo or monuments is often discouraged/fined.
Standing at a café bar for espresso is cheaper than table service — check pricing signs.
localTransportGuide: The historic center is compact and best explored on foot — most major sights are within a 20-minute walk of the Duomo. ATAF buses cover areas outside the walkable core, and trains from Santa Maria Novella station make easy day trips to Pisa, Siena and Lucca.
bestTimeToVisit: April to June and September to October bring warm days, blooming Tuscan countryside and fewer crowds than midsummer.
weather: Mediterranean climate with cool, damp winters (highs 11°C) and hot, humid summers (highs 32°C) that can feel stifling in the enclosed city center.
visaInformation: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities should check Schengen short-stay visa requirements in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~9.9h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Florence?
faqs.answer: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities should check Schengen short-stay visa requirements in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Florence?
faqs.answer: April to June and September to October bring warm days, blooming Tuscan countryside and fewer crowds than midsummer. Mediterranean climate with cool, damp winters (highs 11°C) and hot, humid summers (highs 32°C) that can feel stifling in the enclosed city center.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Florence safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Florence is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Florence?
faqs.answer: The local currency is EUR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Liam Wang
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Our trip to Florence was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Palazzo Pitti & Boboli Gardens was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Anna Patel
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Florence is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Santa Croce Basilica. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Lucas Santos
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Giardino delle Rose and Santa Croce Basilica. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Olivia Santos
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Our trip to Florence was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Medici Chapels was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Elena Patel
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Medici Chapels and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.body: Our trip to Florence was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Palazzo Vecchio was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.
